close_reason: Fixed: record.xml = xml (raw string) instead of Nokogiri::XML(xml). Estimated ~5 min, actual ~2 min. 10 seed helper tests pass.

title: Fix ID type coercion — TriageQueueNav + TriageSplitView
description:
Title: Fix ID type coercion — TriageQueueNav + TriageSplitView

Description:
currentId and activeCommentId are typed as [Number, String] but compared with === to numeric IDs from data. When route params pass string IDs (e.g. "5" from $route.params), strict equality fails and navigation breaks. Normalize all ID comparisons to Number(). Flagged by Copilot review items #5 and #6 on PR #731.
Design doc: PR #731 Copilot comments #5, #6

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js

First failing test:
mount TriageQueueNav with currentId="5" (string), expect position indicator to match rule with id:5

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] TriageQueueNav correctly highlights active rule when currentId is a string from route params
- [ ] TriageSplitView correctly identifies active comment when activeCommentId is a string
- [ ] All === comparisons involving IDs use Number() normalization or == where appropriate
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js

Decision points:
- If IDs are used in Map/Set lookups elsewhere, ask whether to normalize at the data layer instead

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use == for loose comparison — explicitly convert with Number() for clarity
- Do NOT change the prop type definition — keep [Number, String] for flexibility
- Do NOT add parseInt — use Number() which handles edge cases better

NOT in scope:
- Route param typing changes
- Vue Router configuration changes
- ID normalization in other components outside triage

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:1
Estimate: 5 minutes, Claude-pace
close_reason: Fixed: normalizedCurrentId computed in TriageQueueNav, Number() in TriageSplitView. Estimated ~5 min, actual ~3 min. 54 tests pass.

title: Fix factory traits after(:build) DB writes — reply, component_comment, duplicate
description:
Title: Fix factory traits after(:build) DB writes — reply, component_comment, duplicate

Description:
Three review factory traits (:reply, :component_comment, :duplicate) use after(:build) with create() calls inside, making build() non-side-effect-free. This breaks test isolation — calling build(:review, :reply) persists records to the database. Move the create() calls to before(:create) callbacks instead. Flagged by Copilot review items #2/#3/#4 on PR #731.
Design doc: PR #731 Copilot comments #2, #3, #4

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: spec/factories/reviews.rb
- Test: spec/factory_specs/factory_traits_spec.rb

First failing test:
build(:review, :reply) should not persist any records

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] build(:review, :reply) does not persist any records to the database
- [ ] build(:review, :component_comment) does not persist any records to the database
- [ ] build(:review, :duplicate) does not persist any records to the database
- [ ] create(:review, :reply) still works correctly and creates all associated records
- [ ] create(:review, :component_comment) still works correctly
- [ ] create(:review, :duplicate) still works correctly
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/factory_specs/factory_traits_spec.rb && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- If other factories have the same after(:build)+create() pattern, ask whether to fix them in this card or a separate one

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use after(:build) with any database-writing calls
- Do NOT change the behavior of create() — only fix build() side effects
- Do NOT remove the trait functionality, just move the timing

NOT in scope:
- Fixing factory traits in other factory files (only reviews.rb)
- Refactoring factory inheritance structure
- Adding new factory traits

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 minutes, Claude-pace
close_reason: Fixed: reply, component_comment, duplicate traits moved from after(:build) to before(:create). Estimated ~10 min, actual ~3 min. 35 factory tests pass.

Follows the GitLab/ThoughtBot two-concern pattern.

- [ ] seed_xccdf extracted from seeds.rb with identical behavior

- [ ] rake dev:reset clears demo data (by email/name pattern) then re-primes
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

bundle exec rspec spec/lib/seed_helpers_spec.rb && rails dev:status

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use delete_all or truncate without explicit user confirmation
- Do NOT change the VULCAN_SEED_DEMO_DATA env var behavior

title: Add comment count badges + related comments list per section
description:
Title: Add comment count badges + related comments list per section

Description:
Section headers in the rule context panel show a count badge ("Check (3)") indicating how many comments target that section of this rule. When a section is expanded, a compact list of all comments on that section is shown below the rule text, so the triager sees related comments in context — they can identify duplicates and agreements before making a decision. Clicking a related comment switches to it in the queue.

title: Group queue navigation by rule — industry-standard triage pattern
description:
Title: Group queue navigation by rule — industry-standard triage pattern

Description:
Replace flat comment queue with rule-grouped navigation matching established patterns (GitHub groups by file, Relativity by document, DISA's own matrix organizes by rule ID). Comments on the same rule appear together so the triager maintains context. Within a rule, comments are grouped by section. "Save & next" advances through comments within a section, then to the next section, then to the next rule. Progress shows both rule and comment position.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-16-triage-context-panel.md §Phase 2

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.vue (grouped rendering), app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (grouped navigation logic)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js, spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js

First failing test:
TriageQueueNav renders comments grouped by rule_id with rule headers

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Queue nav groups comments by rule_id
- [ ] Each rule group shows rule_displayed_name as a header
- [ ] Within a group, comments are listed by section
- [ ] Counter shows "Rule 1 of N — Comment M of K"
- [ ] Prev/next navigates within rule first, then to next rule
- [ ] "Save & next" advances: next in section → next section → next rule
- [ ] Last comment in last rule + Save & next exits split mode
- [ ] Jump-to dropdown shows grouped structure
- [ ] Single-comment rules don't show redundant sub-grouping
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn vitest run spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js

Decision points:
- If grouping makes the dropdown too tall for 200+ comments, add collapsible rule headers in the dropdown

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT flatten comments back to individual items for navigation — the grouping IS the navigation
- Do NOT sort within a rule group by anything other than section then ID (match DISA matrix order)
- Do NOT change the backend — grouping is a frontend concern computed from existing rows

NOT in scope:
- Batch "triage all as..." for duplicate comments (separate card)
- AI-powered duplicate detection (future)
- Keyboard shortcuts for triage decisions (separate card)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

title: Wire split-pane layout into ComponentComments — integration
description:
**Target: Vulcan v2.x (Vue 2.7 + Bootstrap-Vue 2.13 + Bootstrap 4.6)**

Integration task — biggest card in the epic. Creates TriageSplitView.vue as a new component that owns ALL split-mode state (preventing ComponentComments from becoming a god component). Incorporates: Vue 2 reactivity fix (activeCommentId stored as data, object derived via computed), optimistic locking (updated_at check on save, 409 Conflict handling), dirty-form guard (confirm before switching with unsaved changes), filter-interaction handling (exit split if active comment filtered out), lazy-fetch rule content via conditional include_rule_content param, save button disabled during pending request.

- Test: spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js (extend)

First failing test:

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] activeCommentId is data, activeComment is computed (Vue 2 reactivity safe)
- [ ] Lazy-fetches rule content via ?include_rule_content=true when entering split mode
- [ ] Dirty-form guard: prompts before switching when form has unsaved changes
- [ ] Optimistic lock: sends updated_at on save; handles 409 Conflict with inline alert
- [ ] 422 validation errors surface via AlertMixin (non-concur without response)
- [ ] 403 permission errors surface with structured message (from Plan B)
- [ ] Network failures surface via AlertMixin
- [ ] Save button disabled during pending request (double-click guard)
- [ ] Exits split mode when active comment removed from rows (filter change)
- [ ] col-lg-5 / col-lg-7 layout (not col-md — too narrow at 1280px)
- [ ] ComponentComments delegates to TriageSplitView via v-if (stays lean)
- [ ] Emits triaged and exit events to parent

- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn vitest run spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.spec.js spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js

Decision points:
- If Bootstrap-Vue b-row/b-col layout breaks at 1280px with lg, try min-width fallback
- If paginated_comments round-trip for rule content is too slow, consider caching strategy

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT store activeComment as a data property pointing to a row object (stale reference)
- Do NOT put split-mode state in ComponentComments (god component)
- Do NOT silently overwrite on concurrent triage (must check updated_at)
- Do NOT swallow any error — 422, 403, 409, and network all must surface visibly

NOT in scope:

title: Backfill request_uuid for non-HTTP audit creation paths
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by audit-compliance agent review (Q2).**

The audited gem auto-populates `request_uuid` only inside Rails HTTP requests (via `Audited::Sweeper` Rack middleware). Verified: 3492/5126 (68%) of dev DB audits have NULL request_uuid — all `auditable_type='BaseRule'` from seed/import paths.

## Affected paths

- Rake tasks (e.g. `stig_and_srg_puller:pull`)
- Seeds (`db/seeds.rb`)
- ActiveJob workers
- `Component#duplicate_reviews_and_history` (Ruby method, not request-scoped)
- `ReviewBuilder` import path (uses `Review.insert!` so no audit at all)
- `after_commit` / async hooks dispatched after request ends

## Impact

`AuditEventBundle.bundled_with` (commit `7a7fc2e`) returns just the trigger row when request_uuid is nil — forensic correlation breaks for any non-HTTP-driven multi-row operation.

## Fix shape

In `app/lib/vulcan_audit.rb`, add `before_create :backfill_request_uuid_for_jobs` that pulls from `Audited.store` OR a thread-local set by job middleware (e.g. `ActiveJob::Base.around_perform`).

Document the boundary in `post-merge-remediation-notes.md`: "request_uuid correlation requires either an HTTP request or job middleware that sets the thread-local."

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Job middleware sets `Audited.store[:current_request_uuid]` per job
- [ ] Rake-task helper sets it for long-running tasks
- [ ] before_create hook backfills if unset (uses SecureRandom.uuid for orphans, with a flag column or comment indicating "non-request-scoped")

title: move_to_rule writes outbound audit on source rule
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review.**

`reviews_controller.rb:629-635` `move_review_subtree!` writes one audit per moved review (good), and via `vulcan_audited associated_with: :rule` (`review.rb:48`) the audit is attached to the NEW rule. Source rule has no record of an outbound move.

## Forensic asymmetry

- Reviewer auditing destination rule Z: sees "comment Y moved here" ✓
- Reviewer auditing source rule X: sees nothing about Y leaving ✗

## Fix

Before walking subtree, write `action: 'review_moved_out'` audit on source rule referencing destination rule_id + review_id. Mirror the pattern at `reviews_controller.rb:398` (Component-level audit before destroy).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] move_to_rule writes outbound audit on source rule before walk
- [ ] Audit row carries: source_rule_id, destination_rule_id, review_id, audit_comment
- [ ] Test: source rule's audit feed shows the outbound entry
- [ ] Test: destination rule's audit feed still shows the inbound (existing behavior unchanged)
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Forensic completeness for cross-rule operations.
close_reason: Outbound audit on source rule shipped. 4 new specs, 98/98 reviews_spec green.

title: Two-pass validate_foreign_key for original lifecycle migration FKs
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review.**

`db/migrate/20260429145530_add_lifecycle_columns_to_reviews.rb:24-27` adds 4 FKs (`triage_set_by_id`, `adjudicated_by_id`, `duplicate_of_review_id`, `responding_to_review_id`) inline with column adds. Rails 8 default `add_foreign_key` validates immediately, taking ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on the `reviews` table for the duration of validation. On a populated production reviews table this can lock writes for seconds.

Same anti-pattern as the pre-fix index migration (`.2`) — same Strong Migrations 2-pass remediation.

## Fix

Convert to two-pass per Strong Migrations:
1. Original migration: change to `add_foreign_key … validate: false`
2. New migration: `disable_ddl_transaction!` + `validate_foreign_key :reviews, column: ...` for each of the 4 FKs

Already past initial deploy on `feat/viewer-comments` so this is post-merge cleanup, not blocker.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 4 FKs have `validate: false` on `add_foreign_key`
- [ ] New `disable_ddl_transaction!` migration runs `validate_foreign_key` for each
- [ ] Schema.rb identical net state
- [ ] Verified on fresh test DB
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Post-merge cleanup; not blocker.
close_reason: Completed in session B (commit eef28a7 as kea). Lifecycle FKs split into 2-pass pattern.

title: Add zip-bomb decompression budget to json_archive import
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by security-review agent on `.4` work.** `JsonArchiveImporter` accepts operator-uploaded zip archives. Current controls:

- `100.megabytes` upload cap (`projects_controller.rb:20`) — **pre-decompression only**
- rubyzip 2.4.x `Zip.validate_entry_sizes` defaults true — **per-entry only, not aggregate**
- Whole import wrapped in one `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction` (`json_archive_importer.rb:179`) — DB connection held for entire decompression duration

## Threat

A 50–100 MB JSON-of-empty-arrays archive decompresses to multiple GB before Ruby OOMs. Even non-malicious operator misuse (fat-fingered export of a huge component history) hangs the worker + holds the DB connection.

Trusted-admin model means severity is "noisy worker hang" not "data corruption", but the failure mode is opaque and happens silently until OOM.

## Fix shape

- Iterate archive entries computing `entry.size` (uncompressed) against an aggregate budget (proposed: 500 MB)
- Reject with HTTP 413 + clear toast before parsing any entry
- Add unit spec with a fixture archive that exceeds budget

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `JsonArchiveImporter` enumerates entries, computes total uncompressed size before reading
- [ ] Reject with HTTP 413 + clear error message when over budget
- [ ] Per-archive budget configurable via Settings (default 500 MB)
- [ ] Test: archive exceeding budget rejected before DB transaction opens
- [ ] Test: archive under budget proceeds normally
- [ ] No regression on existing 47 importer specs
notes: Surfaced by security agent review on .4, 2026-05-02. M-effort. Trusted-admin upload context limits severity, but failure mode is opaque/silent.
close_reason: Decompression budget shipped via Settings.import.json_archive_size_budget_mb (default 500 MB). 3 new specs, 51/51 importer specs green.

title: Expand ReviewBlueprint default fields (eliminate frontend refetch)
description:
**Updated 2026-05-02 with cross-validation from Rails-architect + design-review agents on `.4` work.**

`app/blueprints/review_blueprint.rb` default fields = `id, action, comment, created_at, name, triage_status, triage_set_at, adjudicated_at, triager_display_name, triager_imported, adjudicator_display_name, adjudicator_imported` (after `.8` work in commit `fafb71b`).

**Still missing for full frontend self-sufficiency** (per Rails-architect agent Lens 8):
- `rule_id` (CommentTriageModal needs to read it for picker scope)
- `section` (modal renders SectionLabel)
- `responding_to_review_id` (modal needs to know if this is a reply)
- `duplicate_of_review_id` (modal needs to know if this is a duplicate)
- `triage_set_by_id` (forensic queries; today only `triage_set_by_imported_email/name` are exposed for the imported case)
- `author_name` (modal renders blockquote header)
- `author_email` (gated — only when caller is admin tier; mirrors disposition export pattern)

## Why it matters

When triage/adjudicate/section/admin endpoints (`reviews_controller.rb` 8 sites) return `ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash(@review)`, the modal cannot refresh in place — it must refetch the parent table row via `this.fetch()`. Net: every mutation = 2 round trips.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Default fields expanded to include the 7 missing lifecycle fields
- [ ] author_email gated by `options[:include_email]` in the blueprint (mirror disposition export pattern)
- [ ] Watch ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap (vulcan-component-blueprint-default-fields-trap memory): ensure no Project#available_components.select(...) breaks
- [ ] CommentTriageModal can update its own state from the response payload alone (no `this.fetch()` after triage/adjudicate)
- [ ] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes all 7 fields
- [ ] Test: author_email present only when include_email: true option passed
- [ ] Test: existing CommentTriageModal vitest specs pass with the richer payload
- [ ] Manual verification: triage a comment via UI, confirm no follow-up `/components/:id/comments` request fires (DevTools Network tab)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Default fields expanded to include all PR-717 lifecycle fields
- [ ] Watch ComponentBlueprint default-fields trap: ensure no Project#available_components.select(...) breaks
- [ ] Test: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash includes triage_status, section, etc.
- [ ] Test: existing ComponentComments tests pass with the richer payload
- [ ] Frontend can drop the post-event refetch (optional follow-up)
notes:
[2026-05-02] Cross-validated with Rails-architect agent on .4 review. Confirmed silent-incomplete payload forces frontend refetch. Promoted P2→P1.
[2026-05-02] CLOSED — 3 commits on feat/viewer-comments. Both ACs met.

Commits:
  7cb0990  expand ReviewBlueprint default fields (primary deliverable)
  da06857  flatten author_email describe to fit RSpec/NestedGroups limit
  6eece58  refresh row in place after triage/adjudicate (no refetch — frontend follow-up)

Implementation:

Phase 1 — Blueprint expansion (7cb0990):

- Added author_email as conditional field gated by render_as_hash(review, include_email: true) — mirrors disposition_matrix_export include_email pattern
- user_id stays excluded as a public-comment correlation guard (intentional asymmetry — admin-tier triage_set_by_id IS exposed for forensic queries; viewer-tier user_id is NOT to prevent cross-comment author correlation during open windows)
- 10 specs added covering field presence + author_email gating

Phase 2 — Frontend refresh-in-place (6eece58):
- ComponentComments.onTriaged / onAdjudicated now call updateRowInPlace(payload) instead of this.fetch()
- updateRowInPlace finds the matching row by id and merges the response payload over the existing row (preserves rule_displayed_name which is computed in paginated_comments, not in the blueprint)
- Defensive fallback to fetch() when payload missing or row not in current page
- 4 vitest specs added covering in-place update + preservation + fallback

ACs all met:

- [x] author_email gated by include_email option

- [x] Test: existing CommentTriageModal vitest specs pass with the richer payload (40/40)
- [-] Manual UI verification: not yet done — require running the dev server and DevTools Network tab. Vitest covers the no-fetch behavior at the unit level. (Calling out this gap explicitly per project rule "if you can't test the UI, say so.")

Test results:
- 2255/2255 backend specs green (rake spec:parallel, 3:56)
- 2289/2289 vitest specs green (yarn vitest run, 21s)

Now-unblocked: vulcan-v3.x-1dj.39 (P3 — create endpoint return review payload to eliminate post-create refetch — same pattern as this card, scope is the public comment POST flow).
[2026-05-02 manual UI verification] AC checked off via Playwright on the running dev server.

Flow:
- Logged in admin@example.com → /components/8/triage
- Pending comment row #1 (CNTR-01-000001 Check) had "Triage" action button
- Opened modal: byline rendered "Lyda Lang · posted 4/29/2026..." (commenter_display_name path resolved to user.name; no imported badge — correct, user is live on this instance)

- Row #1 transitioned in place from "Pending Triage / Triage" to "Accept / Edit / Close" — full row state refreshed from the PATCH response payload alone

Pre-.20 baseline would have produced 2 GETs to /components/8/comments (load + post-save refetch via this.fetch()). Confirming the .20 deliverable: 2 round trips → 1.

Final AC checkbox: [x] Manual verification: triage a comment via UI, confirm no follow-up `/components/:id/comments` request fires (DevTools Network tab) — DONE.

The recursive `move_review_subtree!` at `reviews_controller.rb:618-624` walks N-deep. A bug like `responses.first&.update!(rule_id: ...)` or "only descend one level" would pass the test.

title: Strengthen idempotent section test: target the controller short-circuit explicitly
description: `spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb:1196-1203` idempotent test trivially passes. Pre-condition `update!(section: 'check_content')` runs WITHOUT `audit_comment` set, so audited gem records 1 audit. The PATCH `section: 'check_content', audit_comment: 'noop'` is a no-op. The assertion `audits.count == before_count` would pass even if the controller wrote an audit when input matches current — Rails `update!(same_value)` triggers no Dirty change, no audit. Test catches nothing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test rewritten to assert controller short-circuited (e.g., response body contains `{idempotent: true}` if added, or assertion that the audit_comment string is NOT in any audit) — proves the test catches a regression where the controller writes a redundant audit
- [ ] Spec passes
close_reason: Idempotent flag surfaced in response. Spec asserts presence on no-change + absence on real change. RuboCop clean, 10/10 section specs green.

title: Prevent audit-laundering chain (admin_destroy → re-import wipes trail)
description: Combines H2 + H4. After `admin_destroy` (`reviews_controller.rb:373-406`) cascades replies, audited gem keeps audit rows for destroyed records (good). But re-import via `Review.insert!` skips audited entirely — re-imported review has no audit record. Malicious admin can destroy + re-import to launder lifecycle history (no triage_set_by audit, no destroy event tied to resurrected row).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] On import, write a Component-level audit record listing imported review external_ids with source archive identifier
- [ ] Test: import a fresh archive — Component has audit row `action='import_reviews'` with external_ids list
- [ ] Test: destroy review, export, import — destroyed-then-restored review's history is reconstructible from Component audit
- [ ] Documented in `docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/` followup notes

[2026-05-01 closed in commit 2db6507]
- ReviewBuilder writes Component-level audit row per import: action='import_reviews', audited_changes={archive_vulcan_version, archive_exported_at, review_external_ids}, comment="Imported N reviews from backup archive (vulcan_version=X, exported_at=Y)".
- Audit row only created when ReviewBuilder receives both component: and manifest: kwargs (test/legacy callers without those kwargs skip the audit).
- JsonArchiveImporter accepts imported_by: kwarg, threads it + manifest into ReviewBuilder.
- Tests: 5 unit specs on ReviewBuilder.write_import_audit + 1 integration test via full importer flow.
- Followup notes: docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/post-merge-remediation-notes.md (covers .2, .9, .10).

- Acceptance: all 4 ACs met (audit row written, external_ids list, source archive identified, documented).

title: Add associated_with: :rule to vulcan_audited on Review (audit-trail recoverability)
description: `app/models/review.rb:43` `vulcan_audited only:` lacks `associated_with: :rule`. All other audited models use it (Rule, Membership, AdditionalQuestion, etc.). After `admin_destroy` cascade, audit rows are orphaned — `auditable_id` points to a deleted Review, no `associated_id` to query through. Federal compliance posture: trail exists but is queryable only via raw SQL. Comment at `reviews_controller.rb:372` acknowledges the gap.

title: json_archive: warn instead of silently dropping triage_set_by/adjudicated_by
description:
**Scope expanded 2026-05-01 (Aaron approved):** original card was "warn instead of silently dropping triage_set_by/adjudicated_by". New scope: preserve original attribution per-review when the User can't be resolved on import.

**Why scope grew:** the agent framing ("warn and proceed with nil FK") still loses WHO triaged on cross-instance restore. Federal compliance audit posture requires the attribution chain to survive. Researched GitLab's post-migration mapping (creates placeholder User records + reassignment join table — overkill for Vulcan's one-shot backup/restore use case) and Discourse's external_id pattern (different problem). For Vulcan's scale: 4 columns on Review carry the original email + name when the User doesn't exist on the target.

**Schema** (4 nullable string columns on `reviews`):
- `triage_set_by_imported_email`
- `triage_set_by_imported_name`
- `adjudicated_by_imported_email`
- `adjudicated_by_imported_name`

**Behavior:**
- Resolve User → set FK as today, leave imported_* nil
- Can't resolve → leave FK nil, populate imported_* from archive JSON
- Display: fall back to imported_* with annotation when FK is nil
- Disposition export: same fallback in CSV cells
- Audit: imported_* columns NOT in vulcan_audited only: list (set once at import, never edited)

**References (consulted before deciding):**
- https://docs.gitlab.com/development/user_contribution_mapping/
- https://docs.gitlab.com/user/import/mapping/

**Touch points:**
- New migration: 4 nullable string columns on reviews
- `app/services/import/json_archive/review_builder.rb`: populate imported_* when resolve_user returns nil but archive has email/name
- `app/blueprints/review_blueprint.rb`: expose imported_* fields + computed triager_label / adjudicator_label
- `app/lib/disposition_matrix_export.rb`: fallback in build_row for "Triaged By"/"Adjudicated By"
- `app/javascript/components/components/CommentTriageModal.vue` + `ComponentComments.vue`: render fallback with "imported, no account" annotation
- Tests: importer (3 specs), display (2 specs), export (2 specs)

Combined with line 16 `:authorize_review_owner` (which only checks `@review.user_id == current_user.id`), a user removed from the project (or whose project visibility was revoked) can still withdraw + update their own pending comments. Same for `update`. The comment block at line 17-19 claims `@project is set` by `set_project_from_review` — that's false for `withdraw`.

title: Inherited-requirements as first-class workflow (PR #717 Task 32)
description:
DISA Vendor STIG Process v4r1 §4.1.15 prescribes the inheritance pattern as: status=Applicable-Does Not Meet + Mitigation field with canonical sentence "This requirement is fully mitigated by [parent-STIG-RuleID]" + Status Justification. Vulcan today writes to the wrong field (vendor_comments), picks the wrong status (auto-overrides to Configurable), and uses internal SV-ids instead of human-readable STIG-IDs. Authors have no UI button for "Mark as Inherited"; commenters get no badge.

Schema:
- base_rules.inherited_external_citation (string)
- base_rules.inheritance_justification (text)

Model:
- Rule#inherited? (satisfied_by.any? || external citation present)
- Validator: inherited → status must be DNM
- Validator: inherited → justification required
- Drop status auto-override at rule.rb:140
- New Rule#mitigation_export_text helper

Export:
- Spreadsheet: Mitigation column carries citation; Status Justification carries justification
- XCCDF: verify DISA placement (likely vendor-specific extension)
- Disposition CSV (Task 29): add Status + Mitigation columns

UI:
- "Mark as Inherited" button + modal (two tabs: from-Vulcan-rule cross-project picker / from-external-STIG)
- Justification textarea required, min 50 chars
- Inherited badge on rule list/editor
- Commenter view: badge + hint "consider commenting on the parent canonical"

PR-717 integration:
- Mark-as-duplicate picker (Task 24): suggest parent canonical when current rule is inherited
- Disposition CSV: add Status + Mitigation columns

Acceptance:
- [ ] Migration adds the two columns
- [ ] Inherited rule with status != DNM is invalid
- [ ] Inherited rule with blank justification is invalid
- [ ] Spreadsheet export emits canonical Mitigation sentence
- [ ] XCCDF export places Mitigation correctly
- [ ] Cross-project parent rule picker works
- [ ] Existing satisfied_by data untouched on migration (no surprise changes)
- [ ] Disposition CSV (Task 29) has Status + Mitigation columns
- [ ] PR-717 commenter UI shows Inherited badge
- [ ] All tests green
close_reason: Replaced by docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/31-inherited-requirements-workflow.md plan file. Per vulcan-comments-as-objects memory, plan files are the canonical PR-717 tracking; bd cards fragment context. Aaron's call (2026-04-30): Task 31 is FOLLOW-UP phase, not in this PR.

title: BP-8: Remove as_json overrides from models
description:
Final cleanup: remove the old serialization code from models now that blueprints handle it.

Models:
- Rule#as_json override (rule.rb:148-181)
- BaseRule#as_json override (base_rule.rb:84-97)
- Component#as_json override (component.rb:90-100)
- Review#as_json override
- Membership#as_json override
- SeverityCounts#as_json override

Keep any as_json that's used by non-blueprint paths (e.g. BackupSerializer, CSV export) until those are migrated too.

Verify no controller/view still calls .to_json or .as_json on these models.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rule#as_json override removed
- [ ] BaseRule#as_json override removed
- [ ] Component#as_json override removed (or gated to non-blueprint paths only)
- [ ] SeverityCounts#as_json removed
- [ ] No grep hits for 'def as_json' on migrated models
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] BackupSerializer and CSV export still work

Actual removal deferred — remaining callers: (1) lib/tasks/stig_and_srg_puller.rake uses as_json.compact for import, (2) ProjectsController index/show still uses to_json(methods: [...]), (3) ApplicationController check_access_request_notifications uses as_json. These need separate migration before the overrides can be deleted.

title: H4: Batch access_request lookup in ProjectsController#index
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/projects_controller.rb:24-33`

**Problem:** Per-project `current_user.access_requests.find_by(project_id:)` is N+1. 50 projects = 50 queries.

**Fix:** Batch: `ar_by_project = current_user.access_requests.where(project_id: project_ids).index_by(&:project_id)` then hash lookup.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone controller fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] access_request_id uses batch hash lookup, not per-project find_by
- [ ] Test: 10 projects generates 1 access_request query (not 10)
- [ ] Existing projects specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
close_reason: Closed

title: H5: Add eager_load to ComponentsController#find rule serialization
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/components_controller.rb:426`

**Problem:** `render json: rules` triggers full `Rule#as_json` without eager loading associations (reviews, satisfies, satisfied_by, srg_rule). After C3 is fixed, the SRG N+1 is gone, but the association N+1 remains.

**Fix:** Add `.eager_load(:reviews, :satisfies, :satisfied_by, :srg_rule)` to the rules query, or use `as_json(skip_merge: true)` for search results that don't need full detail.

**Depends on:** C3 (Rule#as_json fix).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Component find action eager-loads rule associations
- [ ] Test: find with 10 matching rules generates < 10 queries (not 50+)
- [ ] Existing component find specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
close_reason: Closed

title: H2: Add .limit() to unbounded user audit query in UsersController#index
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:15-18`

**Problem:** `Audited.audit_class.where(auditable_type: 'User').map(&:format)` loads ALL user audit records without `.limit()`. Grows unbounded over time. On a mature instance, thousands of records materialized into Ruby.

**Fix:** Add `.limit(200)` before `.map(&:format)`.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Audit query has .limit(200) or similar
- [ ] Test: with 500 audit records, only 200 returned
- [ ] Existing users_controller specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
notes: [2026-04-07] Still needs .limit() on users_controller audit query. Not addressed by Blueprinter migration.
close_reason: Fixed: added .limit(200) to audit query in UsersController#index. Test added.

title: H3: Consolidate Project#details from 9 COUNT queries to 1-2
description:
**Location:** `app/models/project.rb:62-73`

**Problem:** 9 separate `rules.where(status: ...).size` queries. Each goes through `has_many :rules, through: :components` join. Called on every project show page.

**Fix:** Single query: `counts = rules.group(:status).count` for status counts, plus `rules.where(locked: false).where(review_requestor_id: nil).count` etc. Reduces 9 queries to 2-3.

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone model fix.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Project#details uses GROUP BY instead of 9 separate queries
- [ ] Test: details returns same values as before (regression)
- [ ] Test: only 1-3 SQL queries generated (not 9)
- [ ] Project show page specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
notes: [2026-04-07] Still needs GROUP BY consolidation. Not addressed by Blueprinter migration.
close_reason: Fixed: Project#details rewritten with group(:status).count + group(:locked).count + CASE WHEN for review counts. 9 queries → 3. Test verifies ≤4 queries.

title: H1: Optimize check_access_request_notifications (runs every request)
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/application_controller.rb:268-277`

**Problem:** `before_action` runs on EVERY request. Iterates all available projects, calls `can_admin_project?` per project (membership query each), loads access_requests with eager_load per admin project. For admin with 50 projects = 100+ queries before ANY page renders. Also runs on JSON API calls that don't even render the navbar.

**Fix:**
1. Skip for JSON format: `return @access_requests if request.format.json?`
2. Single query: `ProjectAccessRequest.joins(:project => :memberships).where(memberships: { user_id: current_user.id, role: 'admin' }).eager_load(:user, :project)`
3. Or cache result in session with short TTL

**Depends on:** Nothing — standalone fix in ApplicationController.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Does NOT run N+1 per project
- [ ] Skips for JSON format requests
- [ ] Uses single query instead of iterating projects
- [ ] Test: admin with 10 projects generates < 5 queries (not 20+)
- [ ] Test: JSON API requests don't trigger notification check
- [ ] All request specs pass
- [ ] TDD red -> green
close_reason: Closed

title: Restore prev_unconfirmed_email capture for reconfirmation flash message
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:29`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
resource.unconfirmed_email if resource.respond_to?(:unconfirmed_email)
```

**Problem:** This is a no-op — reads `unconfirmed_email` and throws away the value. Compare to stock Devise which does:
```ruby
prev_unconfirmed_email = resource.unconfirmed_email if resource.respond_to?(:unconfirmed_email)
```
and then passes `prev_unconfirmed_email` to `set_flash_message_for_update(resource, prev_unconfirmed_email)` to tell the user "We sent a confirmation link to your new email address" vs "Profile updated successfully."

**Fix:** Either capture to a local and use it for flash messaging, or delete the line entirely if the simplified flash is intentional.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: user changes email → flash says 'confirmation link sent to new address' (not generic 'profile updated')
- [ ] Request spec: user does not change email → flash says 'profile updated'
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] Cross-check with Devise stock RegistrationsController#update to match behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix Slack notification firing on every user update instead of only admin changes
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:70-72`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
if @user.update(user_update_params)
  notification_type = @user.admin ? :assign_vulcan_admin : :remove_vulcan_admin
  send_slack_notification(notification_type, @user) if Settings.slack.enabled
```

**Problem:** Every successful user update fires a Slack notification as either "assign_vulcan_admin" or "remove_vulcan_admin" — regardless of whether the admin flag actually changed. A simple name change broadcasts "User demoted from vulcan admin" to Slack.

**Fix:** Gate on `@user.saved_change_to_admin?`.

**Status:** Fix applied in working tree. Tests NOT yet written.

**Why:** Slack spam damages trust in notifications; false "promoted/demoted" messages confuse ops team.
**How to apply:** Only notify when admin flag actually changed, not on every update. TDD required.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: update name only → no Slack call
- [ ] Request spec: update email only → no Slack call
- [ ] Request spec: toggle admin to true → :assign_vulcan_admin called
- [ ] Request spec: toggle admin to false → :remove_vulcan_admin called
- [ ] Request spec: update name + toggle admin → exactly one Slack call (admin change)
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] No regressions in users_controller_spec
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix polymorphic audit query missing user_type filter in registrations#edit
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:11-12`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
@histories = Audited.audit_class.includes(:user)
                    .where(user_id: current_user.id)
```

**Problem:** `Audited::Audit#user` is a polymorphic association (`user_id` + `user_type` columns). Filtering on `user_id` alone is incorrect for polymorphic data. Today every actor is a User so IDs don't collide, but `VulcanAudit.create_initial_rule_audit_from_mapping` already uses `user_type: 'System'`, which this query would leak if System shared an ID with current_user.

**Fix:** Add `user_type: 'User'` to the where clause.

**Status:** Fix applied in working tree. Tests NOT yet written.

**Why:** Latent bug — breaks the moment any non-User actor shares an ID space with a User.
**How to apply:** TDD: create an audit with user_type='System' and same id; verify it's excluded from results.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: create audit with same user_id but user_type='System' → NOT returned in @histories
- [ ] Request spec: create audit with user_type='User' → returned in @histories
- [ ] TDD red → green
- [ ] users_controller#index has same pattern — verify already correct (filters by auditable_type)
close_reason: Closed

title: Audit and reduce comment density across the repo
description:
Comments have accumulated across the codebase that don't earn their keep — restated code, card/bead-name tags in app files ("# vulcan-v3.x-XYZ: …"), and multi-line WHY blocks on routine fixes. Drive comment density down by deleting comments that don't help a future reader.

Apply the project's commenting rule: default to NO comment; ≤1 line only when a reader would otherwise be confused; multi-line WHY only for hidden constraints the code can't express (concurrent-write race, audit-trail invariant, browser quirk). Card/bead references belong in commit messages, NOT in code.

Categories to target:
- Card/bead-name comments in app/controllers, app/models, app/javascript, db/migrate, config/routes.rb (search for 'vulcan-v3.x-' or '(card-id)' patterns in code)
- Restated-code comments ('# loop through users', '# spread before sort')
- Multi-line WHY blocks on simple bug fixes / single-line changes
- Per-section / per-method banner comments that add no information

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Sweep results in net comment-line reduction across app/, db/migrate/, config/, doc/
- [ ] No bead/card-name strings remain in code comments
- [ ] Comments that DO survive earn their keep: explain a hidden constraint, point at an upstream bug/issue, or document a non-obvious tradeoff
- [ ] Tests still pass (parallel_rspec spec/ + yarn test:unit)
- [ ] RuboCop + ESLint clean

Verification:
bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/ && yarn test:unit
grep -rn 'vulcan-v3\.x-' app/ db/migrate/ config/ doc/ || echo 'clean'

NOT in scope:
- Documentation in docs/ (separate effort if needed)
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md files
- Changing code behavior — comment-only edits

title: Fix API review findings — mock pollution, CSRF consolidation, JSDoc, param naming, edge case tests
description:

Description:
Expert 3-agent review of the API layer found 6 medium/low issues: ComponentComments test mock pollution, FormMixin CSRF redundancy, missing JSDoc on 81 functions, inconsistent param naming (payload vs data), no null/empty edge case tests, and inconsistent mockResolvedValue vs mockResolvedValueOnce usage. All are quality/maintainability issues — no functional bugs.

(3) Added JSDoc @param/@returns to all 49 functions across componentsApi/projectsApi/rulesApi. (4) Added FormMixin CSRF deprecation comment explaining esbuild pack isolation. (5) Added 3 edge case tests. 104 API specs + 61 ComponentComments specs pass. ESLint + build clean.

title: Replace CommentQueryService .ids materialization with subqueries — reduce memory allocation
description:
Title: Replace CommentQueryService .ids materialization with subqueries — reduce memory allocation

Description:
CommentQueryService#serialize_rows (line 118) calls @component.rules.ids which materializes all rule IDs into a Ruby array, then passes to RuleSatisfaction.where(rule_id: ids). For a 300-rule component, this is 300 integers loaded into Ruby memory and sent back to PostgreSQL as an IN(...) list. Replace with @component.rules.select(:id) subquery — PostgreSQL handles it server-side without round-tripping IDs through Ruby.
Design doc: N/A — from v2.3.1+ performance regression analysis

Files:
- Modify: app/services/comment_query_service.rb
- Test: spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb — 'serialize_rows uses subquery for rule_satisfactions lookup'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] @component.rules.ids replaced with @component.rules.select(:id) subquery
- [ ] RuleSatisfaction and Review child-count queries use subquery instead of IN(...) array
- [ ] Response data unchanged
- [ ] Eliminates 1 materialization query
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- None expected

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use .pluck(:id) — that also materializes; use .select(:id) for subquery
- Do NOT change the response shape

NOT in scope:
- Caching rule IDs across requests
- Changing the pagination logic

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~5 min. Replaced @component.rules.ids with rule_id_subquery in RuleSatisfaction lookup. Eliminates materialization of 300+ rule IDs into Ruby array — PostgreSQL handles it server-side via IN(SELECT ...). 13 CQS specs pass. RuboCop clean.

title: Add error path tests to all 9 API test files — rejected promises, network errors
description:
Title: Add error path tests to all 9 API test files — rejected promises, network errors

Description:
All 71 existing API tests only cover the success path. Zero tests use mockRejectedValue(). This means the API layer's error propagation is completely unverified — if a function swallows an error or transforms it incorrectly, no test catches it. Add error path tests for at least one function per module (9 tests minimum) plus edge case tests for null/empty params.
Design doc: N/A

Files:
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/authApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/baseApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/componentsApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/membershipsApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/projectsApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/reviewsApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/rulesApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/searchApi.spec.js
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/usersApi.spec.js

First failing test:
spec/javascript/api/rulesApi.spec.js — 'updateRule propagates rejected promise to caller'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Each of 9 API test files has at least 1 error path test using mockRejectedValue
- [ ] Tests verify errors propagate (are NOT swallowed)
- [ ] At least 3 edge case tests: empty array params, null optional params, missing required params
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/api/ && grep -c 'mockRejectedValue\|rejects' spec/javascript/api/*.spec.js

Decision points:
- If any function silently catches errors (found during testing), flag it as a bug

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT test that the mock was rejected — test that the CALLER receives the rejection
- Do NOT add try/catch to API functions just to make error tests pass

NOT in scope:
- Component-level error handling tests
- Backend error response format changes

Both files need: mock triageService + reviewsApi, replace each axios.patch/delete with the correct service function call, fix URL-based assertions to function-based assertions. 2830/2830 green.

title: Fix table responsiveness — mobile-first layout for all b-table pages
description:
Title: Fix table responsiveness — mobile-first layout for all b-table pages

Description:
All b-table pages (Projects, STIGs, SRGs, Released Components, Users, Triage) render poorly on smaller viewports. Columns overflow, text truncates without indication, and horizontal scrolling is required. Bootstrap-Vue's b-table has built-in stacked mode (stacked="md") but it's not used consistently. Need to research mobile-first table best practices (Bootstrap-Vue stacked, responsive wrapper, column priority/hiding) and apply a consistent pattern across all table pages.

Research needed:
- Bootstrap-Vue b-table stacked="md" vs responsive wrapper
- Bootstrap 5 responsive table patterns
- Tailwind/Nuxt UI table responsive patterns
- Column priority: which columns to show/hide at breakpoints
- Whether to use horizontal scroll, stacked cards, or column hiding

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/security_requirements_guides/SecurityRequirementsGuidesTable.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/projects/ProjectsTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/CommentTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Test: Playwright viewport resize verification at 768px, 576px, 375px

First failing test:
Playwright: navigate to /projects at 576px viewport width — table should not require horizontal scroll

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Research completed: documented approach in card notes before implementing
- [ ] All b-table instances use consistent responsive pattern (stacked or responsive wrapper)
- [ ] Projects table readable at 768px (tablet) and 576px (phone)
- [ ] STIGs/SRGs table readable at 768px and 576px
- [ ] Triage comment table readable at 768px
- [ ] Low-priority columns hidden at small breakpoints (e.g., Description, Last Updated)
- [ ] No horizontal overflow at any standard breakpoint
- [ ] Playwright verified at 1280px, 768px, and 576px viewport widths
- [ ] Dark mode appearance maintained at all breakpoints
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
Playwright viewport resize to 768px + 576px on /projects, /stigs, /srgs — no horizontal scroll, content readable

Decision points:
- Whether to use stacked="md" (cards layout) or responsive (scroll wrapper) or column hiding
- Which columns to hide at each breakpoint — consult with user
- Whether to add a column visibility toggle for users to customize

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT just add overflow-x: auto and call it done — that's a band-aid
- Do NOT hide essential columns without user feedback on priority

title: Fix sidebar + text-muted + link colors — component surface differentiation
description:

Description:

(2) Shiki syntax highlighter always renders with github-light theme — the github-dark theme is loaded but never used. highlightCode() defaults to "github-light" and the fallback hardcodes style="background-color:#fff". (3) MarkdownTextarea.vue has hardcoded .shiki { background-color: #f6f8fa !important } that overrides dark mode. (4) Editor page surface hierarchy is inconsistent — filter panels, toolbar, sidebar, and content area all blend together.

Research:

- Nuxt UI v4 bg hierarchy: bg (900) → bg-muted (800) → bg-elevated (800) → bg-accented (700)
- Material Design M2 surface elevation: body (darkest) → cards/panels (+5-7% white) → elevated (+12%)

title: Fix badge + alert dark mode colors — desaturated per Material Design
description:
Title: Fix badge + alert dark mode colors — desaturated per Material Design

Description:
Bootstrap 4 badges (.badge-warning, .badge-info, .badge-success, etc.) use fully saturated light-mode background colors that are harsh on dark surfaces. Material Design M2 specifically warns against saturated colors on dark: "avoid using saturated colors — they produce optical vibrations against a dark background causing eye strain." Bootstrap 5.3 provides bg-subtle variants (shade-color 80%) and text-emphasis variants (tint-color 40%) for this exact purpose.

Research:
- Material Design M2: "dark theme should avoid using saturated colors" — use 200 tonal value
- Bootstrap 5.3: .badge uses --bs-badge-color and --bs-badge-bg CSS vars. Alert variants use shade-color 80% for bg, tint-color 40% for text in dark mode.
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift 500→400 (one stop lighter, slightly desaturated)
- Color theory: fully saturated yellow (#ffc107) on dark gray (#212529) causes halation — the bright color bleeds at edges

Alert dark mode already partially handled (fad.2 added alert overrides with rgba). This card focuses on BADGES specifically.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (.badge-* overrides in [data-bs-theme="dark"])
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (assertions for badge selectors)

First failing test:
spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb — 'has dark override for .badge-warning' — asserts [data-bs-theme="dark"] .badge-warning has background-color declaration that is not the raw #ffc107

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] .badge-warning bg adjusted — use mix(white, $warning, 20%) bg with dark text, or rgba($warning, 0.2) bg with tinted text
- [ ] .badge-info bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-success bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-danger bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-secondary bg adjusted (already dim — may need lightening not darkening)
- [ ] Playwright: badge-warning computed bg ≠ rgb(255, 193, 7) on /projects table
- [ ] Playwright: all badge text passes WCAG AA 4.5:1 against badge bg
- [ ] Playwright: visually verify "18 pending" badges on /projects are readable without eye strain
- [ ] Light mode badges unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright verify badges on /projects + /components/29/triage

Decision points:
- Whether to use opaque desaturated bg (Material pattern) or translucent bg with tinted text (BS5.3 subtle pattern)
- Whether .badge-primary needs adjustment (blue on dark is usually fine)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use fully saturated colors on dark backgrounds (Material Design rule)
- Do NOT change badge text to white-on-saturated — that's the light mode pattern
- Do NOT hardcode hex — use Sass mix() or rgba()

NOT in scope:
- Triage status badges (those use --triage-* CSS vars from the design system — separate layer)
- Alert adjustments (already done in fad.2)
- CAT severity badges (separate card if needed)

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated

- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Bootstrap 5.3 uses rgba(255,255,255,0.75) (55% increase). (2) Table thead th has no background differentiation from body rows in dark mode. title: Fix outline button colors — tint-color 40% per Bootstrap 5.3
description:
Title: Fix outline button colors — tint-color 40% per Bootstrap 5.3

Description:
Bootstrap 4's .btn-outline-secondary uses text/border color #6c757d ($secondary) which has ~2.5:1 contrast on dark surfaces — fails WCAG AA. Bootstrap 5.3 solves this with tint-color($color, 40%) for text-emphasis dark variants. We need to override every .btn-outline-* variant under [data-bs-theme="dark"] using mix(white, $color, 40%) (BS4 equivalent of tint-color).

Research:
- Bootstrap 5.3 _variables-dark.scss: $secondary-text-emphasis-dark = tint-color($secondary, 40%) → #a7acb1
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift from 500→400 in dark mode (same direction — lighter)
- Material Design M2: use 200 tonal value for primary on dark surfaces (lighter, desaturated)
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum contrast for body text

Computed values (mix(white, $color, 40%)):
- secondary: #6c757d → #a7acb1
- primary: #007bff → #66a9ff
- success: #28a745 → #6dc486
- danger: #dc3545 → #e97a84
- warning: #ffc107 → #ffd45a
- info: #17a2b8 → #5bbfcf

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (add .btn-outline-* overrides in [data-bs-theme="dark"] block)
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (new assertions for outline button selectors)

First failing test:

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] .btn-outline-secondary text/border uses mix(white, $secondary, 40%) ≈ #a7acb1 in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-primary text/border uses mix(white, $primary, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-success text/border uses mix(white, $success, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-danger text/border uses mix(white, $danger, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-warning text/border uses mix(white, $warning, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-info text/border uses mix(white, $info, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] Playwright: computed color on .btn-outline-secondary ≠ rgb(108, 117, 125) in dark mode
- [ ] Playwright: visually verify on /projects/6 (has outline buttons for Members, Triage, Download, Details, etc.)
- [ ] Light mode outline buttons unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright computed-style check on /projects/6 .btn-outline-secondary

Decision points:

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode hex values — use mix(white, $color, 40%) Sass formula
- Do NOT guess what the colors look like — verify in Playwright

NOT in scope:

title: Fix body + foundation dark mode — body bg/color override
description:
Title: Fix body + foundation dark mode — body bg/color override

Description:
Bootstrap 4 compiles body { background-color: #fff; color: #212529 } as hardcoded values. The [data-bs-theme="dark"] block sets these on html, but body paints over html. Bootstrap 5.3 solves this by using CSS variables in body (body { background-color: var(--bs-body-bg) }). Since BS4 can't do that, we explicitly override body under [data-bs-theme="dark"].

Research: Bootstrap 5.3 _reboot.scss body rule uses var(--bs-body-bg). Material Design M2 specifies #121212 as base dark surface. We use Bootstrap's $gray-900 (#212529) which is the same value BS5.3 uses for --bs-body-bg-dark.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (add body rule inside [data-bs-theme="dark"])
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (new assertions for body selector)

- [ ] [data-bs-theme="dark"] body has background-color override (not just html)
- [ ] [data-bs-theme="dark"] body has color override
- [ ] Playwright foundation check passes: body bg = rgb(33, 37, 41), body color = rgb(222, 226, 230)
- [ ] Foundation check passes on at least 3 different pages (/projects, /components/:id, /components/:id/triage)
- [ ] Light mode body bg still white (regression check)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

title: [EPIC] Fix dark mode color correctness — research-backed color adjustments
description:
Title: [EPIC] Fix dark mode color correctness — research-backed color adjustments

Description:
Dark mode was implemented with raw light-mode Bootstrap 4 color values that produce poor contrast, harsh saturation, and invisible elements on dark surfaces. This sub-epic applies research-backed color corrections from Bootstrap 5.3, Nuxt UI v4, Tailwind CSS, and Google Material Design M2/M3. Each card targets one category of color issue with TDD + Playwright verification.

Research references:
- Bootstrap 5.3: tint-color($color, 40%) for text-emphasis, shade-color($color, 80%) for bg-subtle
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift 500→400 in dark mode, text 700→200, bg white→neutral-900
- Tailwind: gray text shifts ~1-2 stops lighter (gray-500→gray-400)
- Material Design M2: use 200 tonal value (not 500-700), desaturate to prevent optical vibrations
- Material Design M2: surface elevation via white overlay (1dp=5%, 2dp=7%, 8dp=12%, 16dp=15%)
- Material Design M2: text opacity hierarchy — high=87%, medium=60%, disabled=38%
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum contrast for body text at all elevation surfaces

Core formula for Vulcan (Bootstrap 4 adaptation):
  mix(white, $color, 40%) = Bootstrap 5.3's tint-color($color, 40%)
  This is the universal dark-mode text/outline/emphasis color adjustment.

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All outline buttons readable on dark surfaces (WCAG AA 4.5:1)
- [ ] Navbar links legible (opacity raised from 0.5 to 0.75+)
- [ ] Table headers visually differentiated from body rows
- [ ] Badge colors appropriate brightness for dark surfaces
- [ ] Text-muted readable on dark bg (gray-400 not gray-600)
- [ ] Sidebar visually differentiated from main content
- [ ] Every page Playwright-verified in dark mode (foundation check passes)
- [ ] Light mode completely unchanged (regression check)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests
- [ ] /dark-mode-verify skill executed on every change

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright visual review of all 9 page types in both modes

Decision points:
- Whether solid buttons (btn-primary, btn-danger) need any adjustment (BS5.3 says no — verify)
- Whether to adjust badge bg-color or just text-color for dark mode

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT write CSS without verifying selectors match real DOM elements (querySelector first)
- Do NOT close cards without Playwright computed-style + screenshot verification
- Do NOT use saturated/vibrant colors on dark surfaces (causes optical vibrations per Material Design)
- Do NOT change light mode appearance
- Do NOT guess color values — use mix(white, $color, 40%) formula from BS5.3 research

NOT in scope:
- Bootstrap 5 migration
- Vue 3 migration
- Color palette redesign or rebranding
- Per-user theme preference in database
- Login/auth page dark mode (separate pack)
- Animation or transition effects for mode switching

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] All child cards closed with Playwright evidence

description:
Title: Add "Overall Requirement" visual treatment in rule context panel

Description:
When a comment targets the overall requirement (not a specific section), the right panel says "Section: Overall Requirement" but the middle panel has no corresponding visual treatment. Section-specific comments get a blue focus tint on their targeted section. Overall comments need an equivalent — a subtle visual cue on the rule description area showing "this comment is about the whole requirement."

- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/RuleContextPanel.spec.js

- [ ] Visual indicator in middle panel when comment targets overall requirement

- [ ] Does not render when focusedSection targets a specific section

- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

title: Investigate whether fully anonymized exports of Vulcan projects are desirable
description:
Backup/archive exports embed commenter, triager, and adjudicator attribution (name AND email) per review in backup_serializer.rb:192-211 — independent of membership data. Excluding membership does NOT anonymize comments; imported attribution reappears via commenter_imported_name/email.

Investigate whether an anonymized export mode is desirable (e.g. for sharing archives outside the originating org, or public-comment-review windows where PII scraping is a concern). Decisions to make: export-side redaction flag vs import-side stripping; replacement style (generic role tokens, stable pseudonyms, or fully blank). Note the triage UI already withholds email from row payloads but names are shown and emails persist in the DB.

title: Add triage response templates — reusable canned responses
description:
Title: Add triage response templates — reusable canned responses

Description:
Triagers often give the same response to similar comments. In the Container SRG, Aaron and Eugene both wrote "we will generalize the check and fix text" multiple times. Add a response template system where triagers can save, reuse, and share canned responses. Templates are project-scoped (shared with project members). Selectable from a dropdown in the triage form.

title: Add duplicate cluster detection — flag near-identical comments for batch triage
description:
Title: Add duplicate cluster detection — flag near-identical comments for batch triage

Description:
68% of Container SRG comments (79 of 116) are near-identical duplicates posted across multiple requirements by the same commenter. The system should auto-detect these clusters and surface them to the triager as "X similar comments from Y — triage as batch?" This uses simple text similarity (first N characters match + same author), not ML. Surfaces as a badge or section in the comments view showing clustered groups the triager can expand and batch-process.

Files:
- Modify: app/models/component.rb (add comment_clusters method)
- Modify: app/blueprints/component_blueprint.rb (expose clusters in show view)
- Create: app/javascript/components/triage/DuplicateClusterPanel.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.vue (render cluster panel)
- Test: spec/models/component_spec.rb
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/DuplicateClusterPanel.spec.js

First failing test:
it('groups comments with identical first 80 characters from same author into clusters')

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] component.comment_clusters returns groups of 2+ comments with matching text prefix (80 chars) + same user
- [ ] Cluster panel shows at top of comments view: "{N} duplicate clusters detected"
- [ ] Each cluster is expandable showing: author, shared text preview, count, list of rules
- [ ] "Bulk Triage Cluster" button pre-selects all comments in the cluster
- [ ] "Merge Cluster" button opens merge modal pre-populated with cluster members
- [ ] Clusters update when comments are triaged/merged (removed from cluster view)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/component_spec.rb && yarn test:unit -- --grep "DuplicateCluster"

Decision points:
- Text similarity threshold: exact first-80-chars match vs fuzzy? Start exact, add fuzzy later.
- Should clusters span across rules or only same-author same-text?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use NLP/ML — simple text prefix matching is sufficient for this pattern
- Do NOT auto-merge clusters — only surface them for admin review
- Do NOT compute clusters on every page load — cache or compute on demand

NOT in scope:
- Cross-commenter similarity detection
- ML-based semantic similarity
- Auto-triage of detected clusters

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min Claude-pace
notes: [2026-05-21] UX Research: Use simple text prefix matching (first 80 chars + same author) — not ML. Surface as inline banner above comments list: 'N duplicate clusters detected'. Each cluster expandable showing author, text preview, count, affected rules. One-click 'Bulk Triage' or 'Merge' from cluster view. Linear-inspired placement.

Title: Investigate and improve in-component search — requirements editor

- [ ] Search results highlight matching text

title: Add #rule-id linking + @member mentions — comment composer
description:
Title: Add #rule-id linking + @member mentions — comment composer

Description:
When commenting or replying in the triage view, typing # should open a picker of the component's rules so the commenter can reference another requirement (e.g., "We addressed this in #CNTR-00-000050"). Typing @ should open a picker of project members for callouts. Both render as clickable links in the comment display. Bug #1 from the user's list. This is a standard collaborative editing pattern (GitHub issues, Slack, Linear).

Files:
- Create: app/javascript/components/shared/MentionPicker.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/CommentTriageForm.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.vue (render linked mentions)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/shared/MentionPicker.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/CommentTriageForm.spec.js

First failing test:
it('opens rule picker dropdown when # is typed in comment textarea')

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Typing # in comment textarea opens a searchable rule picker showing component rules
- [ ] Selecting a rule inserts a linked reference like #CNTR-00-000050
- [ ] Typing @ in comment textarea opens a searchable member picker
- [ ] Selecting a member inserts an @mention like @Aaron Lippold
- [ ] Both render as clickable links when displayed in comment threads
- [ ] # links navigate to that rule in the requirements editor
- [ ] Picker filters as user types after the trigger character
- [ ] Esc closes the picker without inserting
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --grep "MentionPicker|CommentTriageForm"

Decision points:
- Research existing Vue 2 mention libraries (vue-tribute, vue-at) before building from scratch
- Decide on the stored format: raw text "#CNTR-00-000050" vs structured markdown "[CNTR-00-000050](/rules/123)"

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT build a custom textarea parser from scratch — research existing libraries first
- Do NOT store rendered HTML in the database — store the reference format, render on display

NOT in scope:
- Email notifications on @mention
- Mentions in non-triage comment views (future card)

Before closing:

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

Estimate: 30 min Claude-pace
notes: [2026-05-21] UX Research: Use vue-tribute (wraps tributejs) — Vue 2.7 compatible, supports multiple trigger chars. Configure # for rules (shows rule_id + title, filterable), @ for project members (shows name + email). Stored as plain tokens (#CNTR-00-000050, @alippold), rendered as clickable links at display time. GitHub pattern.

title: Triage Sonar quality gate failure on PR #717 (Reliability rating C)
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-01 by sonarqubecloud[bot] on PR #717.**

Sonar quality gate failed: "C Reliability Rating on New Code" (required ≥ A). At least 6 reliability issues introduced.

Need to fetch the actual issue list via `mcp__sonarqube__search_sonar_issues_in_projects` or via the URL https://sonarcloud.io/dashboard?id=mitre_vulcan&pullRequest=717. SONARCLOUD_TOKEN env var is available.
